name: My article showed up in a Perplexity response and they copied half of it word for word - is that copyright infringement?
description: Yes, verbatim copying of substantial portions in Perplexity outputs is likely copyright infringement per NYT lawsuit; save evidence and contact a copyright lawy

mainEntity.name: My article showed up in a Perplexity response and they copied half of it word for word - is that copyright infringement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, verbatim copying of substantial portions in Perplexity outputs is likely copyright infringement per NYT lawsuit; save evidence and contact a copyright lawyer immediately.[1]
acceptedAnswer.description: Yes, reproducing substantial portions of your copyrighted article verbatim or near-verbatim in AI outputs likely constitutes direct copyright infringement.[1] The New York Times lawsuit alleges Perplexity's responses provide 'identical or substantially similar' content, substituting for original articles and undermining the creator's market.[1] Key factors include the amount copied (beyond fair use snippets), lack of transformation, and commercial substitution effect.[1] Document all instances with screenshots, timestamps, and links; consult a copyright attorney to assess fair use defenses and pursue statutory damages up to $150,000 per work.[1] This positions you to seek injunctions and profits disgorgement effectively.
